violations.text: Cases of food and beverage items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and back storage areas. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

Cardboard was observed lining the shelves in the walk-in cooler area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the racks in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

A hose is connected to the faucet at the mop sink. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025
A leak was observed in the piping near the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2025

The floors under the retail shelves throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

Various items were observed held between 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the open case refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Cardboard was observed lining the shelves in the walk-in cooler. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Open case cooler in the retail area was observed with an ambient temperature between 44-46 degrees Fahrenheit. Refrigeration units must keep TCS foods 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. PIC took open case cooler out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the milk racks in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

The floors under retail shelves throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the milk cooler (shelving), chip aisle (shelving) and 3 bay sink have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023
Observed condensate line to be draining into 3 bay sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/07/2023
